post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 222:
Stuffed Bus Mafia

"if you're on a swing
and you get hit by a bus
do you stay on the swing?"
(an excerpt)


- Game Rules -
This tab is a summary of the most important rules.

If you are an experienced player: read this, Conduct Guidelines, and the Setup tab.
If you are a new player: At least skim through everything. The most important rules are in the three tabs under Game Rules and the Conduct Guidelines tab.
  1. Do not edit or delete your posts, even for typos. Just post again.
  2. Each "day" phase is 2 weeks in real life while "night" lasts 3 days.
  3. Do not post during night.
  4. You must format your votes correctly. Neither divergent capitalizations of usernames nor capital i/lowercase L are differentiated; manipulations of either still count as valid votes.
  5. Majority hang is off for Day 1, but it is on starting Day 2.
  6. Deadline hang is on from Day 1 but it is off for LYLO.
  7. Votes are locked at LYLO.
  8. Do not OOT (Out of Thread) and talk about this game outside your role PM, QTs, or this thread, even if you are dead. Access to the spectator QT will be granted upon request.
  9. This game uses Dawn start; Day 1 will not start until everyone shows that they have read the rules.
  10. All times will be given in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC -5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC -4), as applicable.
  11. Activity rules for this game are 48 hours to in-game prod and 72 hours to PM prod.
  12. Use common sense.
  13. All other rules are standard. If you break a rule that isn't covered here, you may still be warned.
  14. This game operates optional discord spectator and mafia private chats using the Unpaid Intern Central server. PM the mods to request access.
  15. Best of luck, and have fun!
  • General
    Credits to Binomial-theorem, chaotic_iak, willwang123, and fdas, among others, as well as the Mafia Universe forum rules, for the rule template.

    The following are standard mafia game rules.

    • Do not post during the night phase.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
    • Do not talk about this game outside of this thread to anyone other than the game moderators unless you are specifically given permission to do so.
    • Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread. Referring to anything that has happened in an ongoing game, even if you do not say which game it is, is OOT.
    • Do not use any formatting that obscures or conceals text. This includes [color=#FFFFFF], [color=white], and [color=transparent].
    • Hide tags are allowed as long as they are visible.
    • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted, even for things like typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • If you are dead, do not post in this thread, and do not talk about this game anywhere else. You may still spectate (you will be granted access to the spectator QT).
    • Do not impersonate the mods.
    • Do not lie to the mods.
    • Do not abuse loopholes in the rules. Intent will be taken into account during rule enforcement.
    • You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications. Additional details are in the Moderation Policies tab.

  • Votes and Hangs
    This tab covers voting, unvoting, hangs, and No Hangs.

    • Vote in bold font in this format: Vote: [player username]. A vote will not be counted if it does not follow this exact format.
    • The capitalization of the player name does not matter, but the exact spelling does. Do not use nicknames.
    • The one exception to the above rule is the use of I (capital i) or l (lowercase L). They are considered identical for the purposes of voting.
    • For example, both Vote: mathjay and Vote: mAtHjAy are valid votes, but vote: mathjay, Vote: jay, Vote: mathjayy., Vote:mathjay, Vote: mathjay, and Vote: mathjay are not valid votes.
    • Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags do not count.
    • Unvote by posting Unvote. You do not have to unvote before voting again.
    • Invalid votes will not count under any circumstances. If you want to purposely fakevote, please try to notify the mods in your role PM so that it will not be accidentally counted.
    • Valid votes will always be counted unless it was clearly unintentional. There is no guarantee that an accidental vote will not be counted.
    • This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EOD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged.
    • If two or more players have the same number of votes when the deadline is reached, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Starting Day 2, if a player is voted by the majority of living players, then that player will be hanged immediately.
    • Upon reaching LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), votes will be locked, meaning that they cannot be changed.
    • Deadline hang is off at LYLO. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, resulting in an automatic win for the mafia team.
    • You may choose on any day to not hang anyone by posting Vote: No Hang.
    • If an even number of players are alive, half of them voting for No Hang will be considered a majority. This only applies to No Hang.
    • The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted: votes must be cast before the specified EOD time. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • If the deadline has passed or a hammer has occurred and the moderators have not declared the end of day, the game will proceed into twilight. Players may still post, but no more votes or unvotes will be counted.
    • Once the moderators declare the end of day, the person hanged will be officially dead, and their role will be revealed. Night will then begin immediately.

  • Activity
    This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.

    • All times will be given in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC -5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC -4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
    • Each day phase will last 336 hours (14 days). The moderators may extend the deadline for the end of day at their discretion.
    • Each night phase will last 72 hours (3 days). The moderators may extend the deadline for the end of night at their discretion.
    • During the night phase, players with unsubmitted NAs (night actions) will be sent a reminder PM 36 to 24 hours before the deadline.
    • If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform a NA that night. NA submissions made after the night deadline will be invalid.
    • Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
    • Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night. You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates in the case of a missed deadline.
    • Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence). Please post V/LAs in bold in-thread or in your role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded.
    • V/LAs should be shorter than a week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for more options.
    • Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request replacement by posting Request Replacement in-thread or in their role PM.
    • Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in.
    • Players will receive an in-game prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on what constitutes a "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Players will receive a PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA.
    • Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 108 hours and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement.
    • If a player receives 4 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
    • If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. This will not count as an official prod.
    • If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
- Conduct Guidelines -
This tab summarizes the tabs following this one, containing concepts vital to healthy gameplay.

Important note: If this game is causing you significant stress or frustration, please feel free to use your role PM to talk or vent. While we may not be allowed to give you technical advice, we can talk and listen, or even help you find a better way to cope. You may also contact us via Discord if you prefer; you can ask us about this in your role PM as well.
  1. Do not gamethrow or intentionally sabotage your wincondition.
  2. Do not attach files or link to external documents concerning this game. Discussions must stay in this thread.
  3. Do not use OGI (Out of Game Information). Publicly discussed reads should be made exclusively from information in this thread or past completed games.
  4. Do not expose this rule or you may be expelled. Exhibit your examination of the rules by expressing with explanations your exclusive answer to the exigent excerpt at the top of this post.
  5. Do not use formatting that makes text hard to read.
  6. You may attack a player’s opinion, but do not make personal attacks on players themselves.
  7. First-timers can receive up to 3 warnings before their next offense results in a force replacement or modkill. All other players can receive up to 2.

  • Sportsmanship
    This tab covers concepts that are conducive to healthy gameplay and steps moderators may take to intervene if things go wrong.

    Most players will not encounter the situations detailed below and usually will not be warned even if they do not observe these guidelines.

    This section will be updated periodically. If this happens while a game is in progress, a notice will be posted in the bottom of the OP.

    • Do not gamethrow.

    This includes: hard claiming mafia for no reason, outing your teammates, posting screenshots of the Mafia QT, hanging a conftown as town
    This does not include: giving up, strategic suiciding, lolcatting, self-voting

    You should play towards your wincondition(s) to the best of your ability. Intentionally sabotaging the game for any reason - such as trolling, a personal vendetta, or frustration - is prohibited. Moderator action will vary based on context, but will likely be severe, ranging from a modkill to a forum ban. Note that only intentionally playing against your wincon and hurting your teammates is banned - you are otherwise allowed to play however you like: giving up, being unhelpful, or making mistakes in good faith will not prompt intervention.

    Important note: According to decisions made by forum moderators detailed in this post, anything that appears to be gamethrowing - whether real or fake - is not allowed. Any serious statement along the lines of "I am actually mafia and/or my partner is [player]" that appears to be made with the intent to gamethrow will result in an immediate modkill, even if the statement is false. As this subject requires nuanced knowledge of the difference between lolcatting and gamethrowing, please avoid this type of situation entirely until you have enough experience to navigate the fine line between the two.

    • Publicly discussing player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, and other moderator interactions is discouraged.

    This includes: "[player] was probably force replaced for trying to PM their mafia partner"
    This does not include: discussing lurking or player activity in reference to their alignment

    Seriously discussing a player's alignment based off of their V/LAs, replacements, warnings, or other essential moderator communications is highly discouraged. Speculation, jokes, or hot takes about such events are okay, but if a serious discussion occurs in the thread, the moderators may step in and ask participants to stop. Repeatedly refusing to listen to the moderators may result in a warning.

    • Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive inter-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.

    This includes: an encrypted file only certain players possess the key to, using cryptosystems, using encryption programs to hide messages in posts
    This does not include: leaving breadcrumbs, manually encoded/decoded ciphers, a public riddle, brief conversation in non-English languages

    Unless private day-communication is specifically enabled in the game, you are prohibited from attempting to communicate with other player(s) privately. This is an extension of the rule banning posts with concealed text: all game-related information should be visible and reasonably available to be accessed and/or analyzed by all players. If you do wish to establish a secret method of daytime communication with your teammates, it may be a good idea to run your idea by the moderator team, but even if you do not, you will likely only get a warning or receive a request to cease using the method in question.

    • Avoiding referencing OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or creating OGI situations that could be construed as alignment indicative is recommended.

    This includes: adding all game players to an "unrelated" PM, "[player] is active in their other games, but not this one. Are they lurking as maf?" or "[player] and [player] have been acting unusually friendly in [this unrelated forum], I bet they're actually the scumteam."
    This does not include: mentioning real life, online interactions, other unrelated comments as long as they are alignment agnostic

    OGI (Out of Game Information) is exactly what it stands for - anything that happens outside of the game thread - and it is only problematic when it is used alignment-indicatively. The most important things to remember are, a) do not publicly discuss a read based on OGI, and b) try to avoid situations outside the game thread that could cause other players to suspect your alignment, town or mafia. Everything else is fine, and mentioning things that happened outside the thread or in real life in the game thread is also fine, but it must be alignment agnostic. The purpose of this is to preserve game integrity by minimizing outside influences. The moderators may intervene to stop an OGI discussion or incident. Repeated failure to listen to the moderators may result in a warning.

  • Etiquette
    This tab covers player etiquette, warning limits, and things players should not do.

    • Do not make personal attacks.
    • Do not spam.
    • Other behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning.
    • Abide by AoPS Community Guidelines.
    • Be mature and use common sense in disputes.
    • For minor rule infractions, first-timers can receive up to three warnings, while all other players can receive up to two. It is possible to receive multiple warnings for a single infraction, depending on the severity. If a player breaks a rule after they have received their maximum number of warnings, or if they commit a modkill-level infraction, they will be modkilled or force-replaced.

    In addition, please note the types of posts that are implicitly discouraged by the Mafia! community. If certain criteria are met, players are liable to be warned for these posts. This should have little to no effect on normal player behavior, and has been created to better define the "do not abuse loopholes" rule and to encourage healthier gameplay.

    Discouraged Player Conduct
    This tab provides detailed examples of posts that may result in a warning.

    This section will be updated periodically. If this happens while a game is in progress, a notice will be posted in the bottom of the OP.

    • Posts with formatting that imitates the text indicating that a post has been edited
    • Posts that are blank or otherwise contain no content
    • Unsolicited advertising
    • Multiple posts with identical content
    • Posts containing large blocks of unoriginal text
    • Extended or in-depth communication in code or non-English languages

    Posts in this category typically will not receive a warning unless one or more of the following criteria are met:

    1. The player possesses a history of warnings and/or disruptive behavior.
    2. The posts are excessive in number, scale, or objectionable content.
    3. The posts have caused notably negative reactions from other players.

    Again, the majority of players should rarely, if ever, have trouble with these guidelines. This is simply to better and more transparently enforce the anti-loophole rule and to reduce deliberately provoking behaviors that are not explicitly forbidden by previous rulesets.

  • Moderation Policies
    This tab provides details on how the moderation team will handle notable fringe scenarios.

    • Role Randomization

    When randomizing roles/alignments at the start of a game, the moderators will not “reroll” or otherwise engage in intentional RNG manipulation. As a result, both newbie/newbie and SE/SE scumteams are possible.

    Current RNG Process (Matrix6)
    All randomizations are done by random.org.

    Once the playerlist has been finalized, the setup will be randomized using the Random Integer Generator:



    The numbers correspond to the order in which the 6 possible setups are listed in the Setup tab.

    Once a setup has been chosen, the roles will be listed alphabetically in the following order: VTs, Town PRs, Mafia Goon(s), Mafia PR. This list will be randomized using the List Randomizer and merged with the playerlist, which is ordered by descending rank and then by alphabetical order. The ordered playerlist of each game is in the Alive/Dead tabs.


    • Tiebreaking

    If there is a tie at deadline, the player on whom the last vote was made first will be hanged. For example, if the most recent vote on mathjay was made on Post #2106 and the most recent vote on HannahW was made on Post #2132, mathjay will be hanged in the case of a tie. This solution was chosen to avoid tiebreaking via RNG while simultaneously preventing hang manipulation by a single player unvoting and revoting.

    • Votes cast during deadline

    If votes or unvotes are cast near the EOD deadline, the moderators will reference the official logged post times. These can be found by locating the relevant post at aops.com/community/u[ID of player] and referencing the upper-right corner of the applicable entry.

    Votes posted on or after the deadline will not be counted. For example, if the deadline is at 2:00 PM, votes shown as being posted at 2:00 and 2:01 will not be counted.

    • Game-advancing Posts

    A "game-advancing post" is anything other than an activity post, ie. a post solely made for the purposes of dodging a prod. For consistency purposes, most posts other than "activity post" or "hi i got prodded bye" will be counted in activity checks.

    • Postbans

    Due to clarifications by the AoPS Sheriff, the moderators can no longer proxy posts from players who have been postbanned. As a result, if a player is postbanned, they will be replaced. This may be subject to negotiation, such as if the postban is less than a week in length.

    Please note that we are only prohibited from proxying posts from a postbanned player. If you have trouble accessing AoPS for any other reason, we will do our best to help.

    You may contact the moderators through discord at a_tps_person#5357 or by editing posts in your role PM if you are unable to post on AoPS.

    • Outside Consulting

    If needed, the moderators may contact a forum or other respected mod for help resolving an incident that could seriously impact game integrity. If you are ever involved in such an incident and disagree with the moderators' course of action, you may request the moderators to consult a second opinion, if they have not done so already.

  • Moderator Information
    This tab provides technical information about this game.

    • HannahW and Scipio1 are the moderators of Mafia Game 222.
    • This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be given private keys granting access to the server as appropriate.
    • Red-colored text is used for votecounts, warnings, and other official announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • Blue-colored text is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators.
    • Green-colored text is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes.
    • Current custom shades are: Town flavor green (#00f752), Activity Check blue (#005dff), HannahW's blue (#0b5394), Scipio1's blue (#3d70ff).
    • The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
    • If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days or by the next applicable EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • The moderators may change the rules at any time, but major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OP changelog. Most rule changes will have little effect on normal gameplay.
    • Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules.
    • Major changes to the OP will be made in between games. The Version History tab lists the important changes.
    • The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make final judgement calls on the moderation of this game.

    MG222 OP is based on Overcomplicated RulesetTM (OCRSTM) version 5.
    Current version: 6.1
    Version History
    OCRSTM is written and maintained by the Unpaid InternTM Moderator Team.

Setup
This game is based on the Matrix6 setup. This is a semi-open, 9 player setup.

Before the game starts, the setup will be randomly chosen from 6 possible role combinations:
  • Setup 1: 1 Town Jailkeeper, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
  • Setup 2: 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
  • Setup 3: 1 Town Tracker, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
  • Setup A: 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
  • Setup B: 1 Town Cop, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
  • Setup C: 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
There are a couple other setup mechanics that should be clarified:
  • This game is Dawn start. Once all the players confirm, Dawn will end, Day 1 will start, and the EOD deadline will be 14 days from then.
  • Doctors cannot protect themselves two nights in a row.
  • A Mafia Roleblocker's action takes precedence over a Town Jailkeeper's action.
  • A Mafia Roleblocker can kill and roleblock on the same night.
  • Cops are sane.
  • Mafia do not have daychat.
Role PM's
Bus Driver (Vanilla Townie) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are a Bus Driver (Vanilla Townie).

Hailing from Iowa, you're the typical school bus driver. You drive, you pick kids up, and you cause mini traffic jams every time you do so. From time to time, you find yourself unconsciously humming "The Wheels on the Bus" or the Thomas the train theme song. Overall the job isn't bad, though you may occasionally have the bored urge to stomp on the gas pedal, careen over the potholes, and make the kids scream... (it's only a passing thought--you've never carried it out and, as far as you can tell, never will). Bus driving is steady, it pays decently, and it's simple. In fact, you might decide to stick with it.
At least, those were your thoughts until you saw the wreck of a school bus impaled on the swings. You haven't even been here half a year, and now you might have to leave again if this, this antibus cult thingy ends up destroying all the buses. There goes your money, your job, your benefits package… You must stop them.

Your power lies in your observation skills, your voice, and your vote.

You win when all Mafia are eliminated, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is here. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Bus Mechanic (Doctor) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are the Bus Mechanic (Doctor).

You used to be the average local mechanic in an average-sized town in average suburbs, but as the population boomed, grander and fancier mechanic shops sprang up, and you started to lose business. Although you still had some faithful customers, a few repairs every few months wouldn't do, so when some enterprising individual offered to buy the shop to convert into a restaurant, you assented, albeit reluctantly.
As you were considering your options, you received a curiously specific email offering you a job as a bus mechanic. The pay seemed good, and driving a bus in addition to the usual repair job didn't seem too hard. So you packed your bags, said goodbye to your old mechanic shop one last time, and headed out west, to Wyoming.

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then take in the bus for repairs for the night, thus preventing it from possibly being stolen and destroyed. You can save your own bus, but you cannot do this two nights in a row.

You win when all Mafia are eliminated, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is here. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
No-Longer-Bored Driver (Tracker) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are the No-Longer-Bored Driver (Tracker).

Your job is rather dull. Driving around all day can make one tired, but you couldn't find another job, so you're stuck. Most days, you just end up reading whatever you can get your hands on--novels, newspapers, biographies--you don't care, and you don't usually pay too much attention anyway.
When you received word of buses strangely flying through the air and crashing like meteors you had been in the middle of a mystery novel in which average people discover amazing secrets and catch notorious criminals… it might have gotten to your head. So you've decided to try it out. After all, what's the worst that can happen? The perpetrators aren't dangerous--you think--and your job has been boring you all year long. You're not going to let that chance pass and just stick to some bus driver meetings.

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then discreetly track them and find out who that player visits or targets, if any, that night.

You win when all Mafia are eliminated, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is here. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Reckless Driver (Jailkeeper) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are the Reckless Driver (Jailkeeper).

To be honest, you're probably the only one out of the bus drivers who truly enjoys their job. You're not just satisfied with it, you love it! After all, what's more fun than wheeling in a hulk of yellow metal all over the place all day long? The others don't quite understand your ecstasy, but, undaunted, you always volunteer for the longest routes and the toughest roads. Even the rowdiest kids can't ruin the thrill, because you're here, roaring over the highways, and that's all that matters.

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then whisk them away in their bus for a midnight ride in an attempt to perhaps instill in them your own enthusiasm for buses, thus preventing them from being targeted or targeting anyone that night. You cannot target yourself with this ability.

You win when all Mafia are eliminated, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is here. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Student Driver (Cop) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are the Student Driver (Cop).

Out of all your fellow bus drivers, you like to think you have the brightest foreseeable future. You don't plan on sticking to this job, no. You're just here to make money--enough money to send yourself to a police academy and fulfill your childhood dream of becoming a police officer. You know it might take a while: that's why you started early and have seized every available resource at every turn. And now here's your chance! There are bad guys on the loose, outrageously wrecking without remorse government property, paid for by tax dollars, and, most importantly, your means of income, your only way to make it into the police academy. They must be stopped.

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then put into practice all that you know so far about policing, investigating them and uncovering their alignment. You may also receive a "No result" if your action is prevented from resolving.

You win when all Mafia are eliminated, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is here. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Paranoid Driver (1-shot Bulletproof) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are the Paranoid Driver (1-shot Bulletproof).

You've been paranoid your entire life, anxious, nervous of what is to come. The solution? You overthink things--or maybe that's the problem. You've heard stories of you running to your mother as a young child, worried whether your birthday would really come or whether your friends would really come to the party. At any rate, that might be why you took the job of a bus driver. Something simple, repetitive, un-worrisome, and maybe just a little bit enjoyable. But then, when you hear about the whole stuffed bus fiasco, it all comes back. Your anxiety. You feel the urge to do something, but you're not sure what. Eventually, you decide to install security for your bus. Bus security? You don't care how stupid it sounds: it works, and that's all that matters for you.

You and your bus with its hastily installed security system will be able to survive one attempted nightkill, but since you did not have enough time to fully secure the bus, if you are targeted again after that, you will be eliminated. You are not immune to the daily hang, but your ability cannot be roleblocked. You do not know when you lose your bulletproof vest.

You win when all Mafia are eliminated, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is here. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Member of the Coalition Against Buses (Mafia Goon) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are a Member of the Coalition Against Buses (Mafia Goon).

You loathe buses. Or more accurately you abhor, abominate, despise, detest, execrate, revile, scorn, spurn, and hate them. For what reason? Who knows. Hate is illogical. So here you are, undercover as a bus driver for several months now--such a repulsive career--and it's finally time. The plan of years in making now flies into action:
You'll start small, take over this little town in Wyoming, and eliminate all buses by catapulting them! Catapulting them--it had been such a brilliant idea: this way no one will know where to start looking for you, and you won't have to clean up after yourself. You just need to stay hidden until all resistance has been eliminated. From here, if you succeed, the secret Coalition Against Buses has plans to take over the world and purge all buses… school buses, public buses, ancient buses, toy buses… And you can't wait.

Your partner is [player] ([role]), and you may discuss with them at night in this QuickTopic while you are alive. Together, you may decide who will use the factional kill to eliminate which player each night.

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal is to stay hidden and kill Town before they find and hang you. The game thread is here.
Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Head of the Coalition Against Buses (Mafia Roleblocker) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are a Head of the Coalition Against Buses (Mafia Roleblocker).

When your grudge first started, you aren't sure, but since you were a child you had hated buses. School buses were stuffy and dirty. Public buses were always late. Tour buses? Well you relented and gave them a chance once and ended up getting your face whacked with all sorts of low hanging objects while riding atop it. But the breaking point came when your favorite aunt was run over by a bus… With the money she left you, you started to plot vengeance--against all buses.
You started the secret Coalition Against Buses, located the perfect place from which to launch--a small town just setting up its school bus system--and prepared, prepared, and triple prepared. It's your chance. You can't afford to lose it, so you'll go to any extreme to ensure the successful debut of your Coalition Against Buses. The realization of your dream of a world with no buses is so, so close…

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then break into that person's house, drug them, rendering them unconscious for the night. Thus you will roleblock them and prevent their night actions, if any, from resolving.

Your partner is [player] ([role]), and you may discuss with them at night in this QuickTopic while you are alive. Together, you may decide who will use the factional kill to eliminate which player each night. You can perform both the factional kill and your roleblock simultaneously.

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal is to stay hidden and kill Town before they find and hang you. The game thread is here.
Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

[hide=- Game Rules -]
[b]This tab is a summary of the most important rules.[/b]

[u]If you are an experienced player:[/u] read this, Conduct Guidelines, and the Setup tab. 
[u]If you are a new player:[/u] At least skim through everything. The most important rules are in the three tabs under Game Rules and the Conduct Guidelines tab. 

[list=1][*][b]Do not edit or delete[/b] your posts, even for typos. Just post again. 
[*]Each "day" phase is [b]2 weeks in real life[/b] while "night" lasts [b]3 days[/b].
[*][b]Do not post during night.[/b]
[*]You [b]must format your votes correctly[/b]. Neither divergent capitalizations of usernames nor capital i/lowercase L are differentiated; manipulations of either still count as valid votes. 
[*][b]Majority hang is off[/b] for Day 1, but it is [b]on[/b] starting Day 2.
[*][b]Deadline hang is on[/b] from Day 1 but it is [b]off[/b] for LYLO. 
[*]Votes are [b]locked at LYLO[/b].
[*][b]Do not OOT (Out of Thread)[/b] and talk about this game outside your role PM, QTs, or this thread, even if you are dead. Access to the spectator QT will be granted upon request. 
[*]This game uses [b]Dawn start;[/b] Day 1 will not start until everyone shows that they have read the rules. 
[*]All [b]times[/b] will be given in [b]Eastern Standard Time[/b] (EST, UTC -5) or [b]Eastern Daylight Time[/b] (EDT, UTC -4), as applicable. 
[*]Activity rules for this game are [b]48 hours to in-game prod[/b] and [b]72 hours to PM prod.[/b]
[*][b]Use common sense.[/b] 
[*][b]All other rules are standard.[/b] If you break a rule that isn't covered here, you may still be warned. 
[*]This game operates optional discord spectator and mafia private chats using the Unpaid Intern Central server. PM the mods to request access. 
[*]Best of luck, and have fun![/list][/hide]
[list][*][hide=General]
[i]Credits to [url=aops.com/community/user/93494]Binomial-theorem[/url], [url=aops.com/community/user/89198]chaotic_iak[/url], [url=aops.com/community/user/90250]willwang123[/url], and [url=aops.com/community/user/144193]fdas[/url], among others, as well as the [url=https://www.mafiauniverse.com/forums/threads/27-Forum-Rules]Mafia Universe forum rules[/url], for the rule template.[/i]

[b]The following are standard mafia game rules.[/b]


[list][*]Do not post during the night phase.
[*]Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
[*]Do not talk about this game outside of this thread to anyone other than the game moderators unless you are specifically given permission to do so. 
[*]Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread. Referring to anything that has happened in an ongoing game, even if you do not say which game it is, is OOT.
[*]Do not use any formatting that obscures or conceals text. This includes [verbatim][color=#FFFFFF], [color=white], and [color=transparent][/verbatim]. 
[*]Hide tags are allowed as long as they are visible. 
[*]Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted, even for things like typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary. 
[*]If you are dead, do not post in this thread, and do not talk about this game anywhere else. You may still spectate (you will be granted access to the spectator QT). 
[*]Do not impersonate the mods. 
[*]Do not lie to the mods. 
[*]Do not abuse loopholes in the rules. Intent will be taken into account during rule enforcement. 
[*]You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications. Additional details are in the Moderation Policies tab.[/list][/hide]

[*][hide=Votes and Hangs]
[b]This tab covers voting, unvoting, hangs, and No Hangs.[/b]


[list][*]Vote in bold font in this format: [b]Vote: [player username][/b]. A vote will not be counted if it does not follow this exact format. 
[*]The capitalization of the player name does not matter, but the exact spelling does. Do not use nicknames. 
[*]The one exception to the above rule is the use of I (capital i) or l (lowercase L). They are considered identical for the purposes of voting. 
[*]For example, both [b]Vote: mathjay[/b] and [b]Vote: mAtHjAy[/b] are valid votes, but [b]vote: mathjay[/b], [b]Vote: jay[/b], [b]Vote: mathjayy.[/b], [b]Vote:mathjay[/b], Vote: [b]mathjay[/b], and [b][i]Vote: mathjay[/i][/b] are not valid votes. 
[*]Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags do not count.
[*]Unvote by posting [b]Unvote[/b]. You do not have to unvote before voting again. 
[*]Invalid votes will not count under any circumstances. If you want to purposely fakevote, please try to notify the mods in your role PM so that it will not be accidentally counted.
[*]Valid votes will always be counted unless it was clearly unintentional. There is no guarantee that an accidental vote will not be counted.
[*]This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EOD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged. 
[*]If two or more players have the same number of votes when the deadline is reached, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]Starting Day 2, if a player is voted by the majority of living players, then that player will be hanged immediately. 
[*] Upon reaching LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), votes will be locked, meaning that they cannot be changed.
[*]Deadline hang is off at LYLO. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, resulting in an automatic win for the mafia team. 
[*]You may choose on any day to not hang anyone by posting [b]Vote: No Hang[/b]. 
[*]If an even number of players are alive, half of them voting for No Hang will be considered a majority. This only applies to No Hang. 
[*]The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted: votes must be cast before the specified EOD time. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]If the deadline has passed or a hammer has occurred and the moderators have not declared the end of day, the game will proceed into twilight. Players may still post, but no more votes or unvotes will be counted.
[*]Once the moderators declare the end of day, the person hanged will be officially dead, and their role will be revealed. Night will then begin immediately.[/list][/hide]

[*][hide=Activity]
[b]This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.[/b]


[list][*]All times will be given in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC -5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC -4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
[*]Each day phase will last 336 hours (14 days). The moderators may extend the deadline for the end of day at their discretion. 
[*]Each night phase will last 72 hours (3 days). The moderators may extend the deadline for the end of night at their discretion. 
[*]During the night phase, players with unsubmitted NAs (night actions) will be sent a reminder PM 36 to 24 hours before the deadline. 
[*]If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform a NA that night. NA submissions made after the night deadline will be invalid. 
[*]Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
[*]Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night. You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates in the case of a missed deadline. 
[*]Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence). Please post V/LAs in [b]bold[/b] in-thread or in your role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded. 
[*]V/LAs should be shorter than a week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for more options. 
[*]Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request replacement by posting [b]Request Replacement[/b] in-thread or in their role PM. 
[*]Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in. 
[*]Players will receive an in-game prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on what constitutes a "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
[*]Players will receive a PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA. 
[*]Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 108 hours and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement. 
[*]If a player receives 4 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
[*]If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. This will not count as an official prod.
[*]If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.[list][/hide][/list]
[hide=- Conduct Guidelines -]
[b]This tab summarizes the tabs following this one, containing concepts vital to healthy gameplay.[/b]

[b]Important note:[/b] If this game is causing you significant stress or frustration, please feel free to use your role PM to talk or vent. While we may not be allowed to give you technical advice, we can talk and listen, or even help you find a better way to cope. You may also contact us via Discord if you prefer; you can ask us about this in your role PM as well. 

[list=1][*]Do not gamethrow or intentionally sabotage your wincondition.
[*]Do not attach files or link to external documents concerning this game. Discussions must stay in this thread.
[*]Do not use OGI (Out of Game Information). Publicly discussed reads should be made exclusively from information in this thread or past completed games.
[*]Do not expose this rule or you may be expelled. Exhibit your examination of the rules by expressing with explanations your exclusive answer to the exigent excerpt at the top of this post.
[*]Do not use formatting that makes text hard to read.
[*]You may attack a player’s opinion, but do not make personal attacks on players themselves.
[*]First-timers can receive up to 3 warnings before their next offense results in a force replacement or modkill. All other players can receive up to 2.[/list][/hide]

[list][*][hide=Sportsmanship]
[b]This tab covers concepts that are conducive to healthy gameplay and steps moderators may take to intervene if things go wrong.[/b] 

Most players will not encounter the situations detailed below and usually will not be warned even if they do not observe these guidelines. 

[i]This section will be updated periodically. If this happens while a game is in progress, a notice will be posted in the bottom of the OP.[/i]


[list][*]Do not gamethrow.[/list]

[b]This includes:[/b] hard claiming mafia for no reason, outing your teammates, posting screenshots of the Mafia QT, hanging a conftown as town
[b]This does not include:[/b] giving up, strategic suiciding, lolcatting, self-voting

You should play towards your wincondition(s) to the best of your ability. Intentionally sabotaging the game for any reason - such as trolling, a personal vendetta, or frustration - is prohibited. Moderator action will vary based on context, but will likely be severe, ranging from a modkill to a forum ban. Note that only intentionally playing against your wincon and hurting your teammates is banned - you are otherwise allowed to play however you like: giving up, being unhelpful, or making mistakes in good faith will not prompt intervention. 

[b]Important note:[/b] According to decisions made by forum moderators [url=aops.com/community/p15771679]detailed in this post[/url], anything that appears to be gamethrowing - whether real or fake - is not allowed. Any serious statement along the lines of "I am actually mafia and/or my partner is [player]" that appears to be made with the intent to gamethrow will result in an immediate modkill, even if the statement is false. As this subject requires nuanced knowledge of the difference between lolcatting and gamethrowing, please avoid this type of situation entirely until you have enough experience to navigate the fine line between the two. 


[list][*]Publicly discussing player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, and other moderator interactions is discouraged.[/list]

[b]This includes:[/b] "[player] was probably force replaced for trying to PM their mafia partner"
[b]This does not include:[/b] discussing lurking or player activity in reference to their alignment

Seriously discussing a player's alignment based off of their V/LAs, replacements, warnings, or other essential moderator communications is highly discouraged. Speculation, jokes, or hot takes about such events are okay, but if a serious discussion occurs in the thread, the moderators may step in and ask participants to stop. Repeatedly refusing to listen to the moderators may result in a warning. 


[list][*]Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive inter-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.[/list]

[b]This includes:[/b] an encrypted file only certain players possess the key to, using cryptosystems, using encryption programs to hide messages in posts
[b]This does not include:[/b] leaving breadcrumbs, manually encoded/decoded ciphers, a public riddle, brief conversation in non-English languages

Unless private day-communication is specifically enabled in the game, you are prohibited from attempting to communicate with other player(s) privately. This is an extension of the rule banning posts with concealed text: all game-related information should be visible and reasonably available to be accessed and/or analyzed by all players. If you do wish to establish a secret method of daytime communication with your teammates, it may be a good idea to run your idea by the moderator team, but even if you do not, you will likely only get a warning or receive a request to cease using the method in question. 


[list][*]Avoiding referencing OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or creating OGI situations that could be construed as alignment indicative is recommended.[/list]

[b]This includes:[/b] adding all game players to an "unrelated" PM, "[player] is active in their other games, but not this one. Are they lurking as maf?" or "[player] and [player] have been acting unusually friendly in [this unrelated forum], I bet they're actually the scumteam."
[b]This does not include:[/b] mentioning real life, online interactions, other unrelated comments as long as they are alignment agnostic

OGI (Out of Game Information) is exactly what it stands for - anything that happens outside of the game thread - and it is only problematic when it is used alignment-indicatively. The most important things to remember are, a) do not publicly discuss a read based on OGI, and b) try to avoid situations outside the game thread that could cause other players to suspect your alignment, town or mafia. Everything else is fine, and mentioning things that happened outside the thread or in real life in the game thread is also fine, but it must be alignment agnostic. The purpose of this is to preserve game integrity by minimizing outside influences. The moderators may intervene to stop an OGI discussion or incident. Repeated failure to listen to the moderators may result in a warning.[/hide]

[*][hide=Etiquette]
[b]This tab covers player etiquette, warning limits, and things players should not do.[/b]


[list][*]Do not make personal attacks.
[*]Do not spam. 
[*]Other behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning. 
[*]Abide by AoPS Community Guidelines. 
[*]Be mature and use common sense in disputes. 
[*]For minor rule infractions, first-timers can receive up to three warnings, while all other players can receive up to two. It is possible to receive multiple warnings for a single infraction, depending on the severity. If a player breaks a rule after they have received their maximum number of warnings, or if they commit a modkill-level infraction, they will be modkilled or force-replaced.[/list]

In addition, please note the types of posts that are implicitly discouraged by the Mafia! community. If certain criteria are met, players are liable to be warned for these posts. This should have little to no effect on normal player behavior, and has been created to better define the "do not abuse loopholes" rule and to encourage healthier gameplay. 

[hide=Discouraged Player Conduct]
[b]This tab provides detailed examples of posts that may result in a warning.[/b]

[i]This section will be updated periodically. If this happens while a game is in progress, a notice will be posted in the bottom of the OP.[/i]


[list][*]Posts with formatting that imitates the text indicating that a post has been edited
[*]Posts that are blank or otherwise contain no content
[*]Unsolicited advertising
[*]Multiple posts with identical content
[*]Posts containing large blocks of unoriginal text
[*]Extended or in-depth communication in code or non-English languages[/list]

Posts in this category typically will not receive a warning unless one or more of the following criteria are met: 


[list=1][*]The player possesses a history of warnings and/or disruptive behavior.
[*]The posts are excessive in number, scale, or objectionable content.
[*]The posts have caused notably negative reactions from other players.[/list]

Again, the majority of players should rarely, if ever, have trouble with these guidelines. This is simply to better and more transparently enforce the anti-loophole rule and to reduce deliberately provoking behaviors that are not explicitly forbidden by previous rulesets.[/hide][/hide]

[*][hide=Moderation Policies]
[b]This tab provides details on how the moderation team will handle notable fringe scenarios.[/b]


[list][*]Role Randomization[/list]

When randomizing roles/alignments at the start of a game, the moderators will not “reroll” or otherwise engage in intentional RNG manipulation. As a result, both newbie/newbie and SE/SE scumteams are possible. 

[hide=Current RNG Process (Matrix6)]
All randomizations are done by [url]random.org[/url]. 

Once the playerlist has been finalized, the setup will be randomized using the [url=https://www.random.org/integers/]Random Integer Generator[/url]: 

[img]https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/708770843731165224/762748231598997534/unknown.png[/img]

The numbers correspond to the order in which the 6 possible setups are listed in the Setup tab. 

Once a setup has been chosen, the roles will be listed alphabetically in the following order: VTs, Town PRs, Mafia Goon(s), Mafia PR. This list will be randomized using the [url=https://www.random.org/lists/]List Randomizer[/url] and merged with the playerlist, which is ordered by descending rank and then by alphabetical order. The ordered playerlist of each game is in the Alive/Dead tabs.[/hide]


[list][*]Tiebreaking[/list]

If there is a tie at deadline, the player on whom the last vote was made first will be hanged. For example, if the most recent vote on mathjay was made on Post #2106 and the most recent vote on HannahW was made on Post #2132, mathjay will be hanged in the case of a tie. This solution was chosen to avoid tiebreaking via RNG while simultaneously preventing hang manipulation by a single player unvoting and revoting. 


[list][*]Votes cast during deadline[/list]

If votes or unvotes are cast near the EOD deadline, the moderators will reference the official logged post times. These can be found by locating the relevant post at aops.com/community/u[ID of player] and referencing the upper-right corner of the applicable entry. 

Votes posted on or after the deadline will not be counted. For example, if the deadline is at 2:00 PM, votes shown as being posted at 2:00 and 2:01 will not be counted. 


[list][*]Game-advancing Posts[/list]

A "game-advancing post" is anything other than an activity post, ie. a post solely made for the purposes of dodging a prod. For consistency purposes, most posts other than "activity post" or "hi i got prodded bye" will be counted in activity checks. 


[list][*]Postbans[/list]

Due to clarifications by the AoPS Sheriff, the moderators can no longer proxy posts from players who have been postbanned. As a result, if a player is postbanned, they will be replaced. This may be subject to negotiation, such as if the postban is less than a week in length. 

Please note that we are only prohibited from proxying posts from a postbanned player. If you have trouble accessing AoPS for any other reason, we will do our best to help. 

You may contact the moderators through discord at [b]a_tps_person#5357[/b] or by editing posts in your role PM if you are unable to post on AoPS. 


[list][*]Outside Consulting[/list]

If needed, the moderators may contact a forum or other respected mod for help resolving an incident that could seriously impact game integrity.  If you are ever involved in such an incident and disagree with the moderators' course of action, you may request the moderators to consult a second opinion, if they have not done so already.[/hide]

[*][hide=Moderator Information]
[b]This tab provides technical information about this game.[/b]


[list][*]HannahW and Scipio1 are the moderators of Mafia Game 222. 
[*]This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be given private keys granting access to the server as appropriate. 
[*][color=#FF0000]Red-colored text[/color] is used for votecounts, warnings, and other official announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
[*][color=#0000FF]Blue-colored text[/color] is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators. 
[*][color=#00FF00]Green-colored text[/color] is used for town flavor-related posts.
[*]Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes. 
[*]Current custom shades are: Town flavor green ([color=#00f752]#00f752[/color]), Activity Check blue ([color=#005dff]#005dff[/color]), HannahW's blue ([color=#0b5394]#0b5394[/color]), Scipio1's blue ([color=#3d70ff]#3d70ff[/color]).
[*]The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
[*]If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days or by the next applicable EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect votecount will become official.
[*]The moderators may change the rules at any time, but major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OP changelog. Most rule changes will have little effect on normal gameplay. 
[*]Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules. 
[*]Major changes to the OP will be made in between games. The Version History tab lists the important changes. 
[*]The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make final judgement calls on the moderation of this game. [/list]

[b]MG222 OP is based on Overcomplicated Ruleset[sup]TM[/sup] (OCRSTM) version 5.[/b]
[i]Current version: 6.1[/i]
[hide=Version History]
[i]OCRSTM is written and maintained by the Unpaid Intern[sup]TM[/sup] Moderator Team. [/i]

[hide=Setup]This game is based on the [url=https://wiki.mafiascum.net/index.php?title=Matrix6]Matrix6[/url] setup. This is a semi-open, 9 player setup.

Before the game starts, the setup will be randomly chosen from 6 possible role combinations:
[list][*][b]Setup 1:[/b] 1 Town Jailkeeper, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
[*][b]Setup 2:[/b] 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
[*][b]Setup 3:[/b] 1 Town Tracker, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
[*][b]Setup A:[/b] 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
[*][b]Setup B:[/b] 1 Town Cop, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
[*][b]Setup C:[/b] 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons[/list]
There are a couple other setup mechanics that should be clarified: 
[list][*]This game is Dawn start. Once all the players confirm, Dawn will end, Day 1 will start, and the EOD deadline will be 14 days from then. 
[*]Doctors cannot protect themselves two nights in a row.
[*]A Mafia Roleblocker's action takes precedence over a Town Jailkeeper's action.
[*]A Mafia Roleblocker can kill and roleblock on the same night.
[*]Cops are sane.
[*]Mafia do not have daychat.[/list]
[hide=Role PM's][quote="Bus Driver (Vanilla Townie)"]Hello, [player]!

You are a [color=#00f752]Bus Driver (Vanilla Townie)[/color]. 

Hailing from Iowa, you're the typical school bus driver. You drive, you pick kids up, and you cause mini traffic jams every time you do so. From time to time, you find yourself unconsciously humming "The Wheels on the Bus" or the Thomas the train theme song. Overall the job isn't bad, though you may occasionally have the bored urge to stomp on the gas pedal, careen over the potholes, and make the kids scream... (it's only a passing thought--you've never carried it out and, as far as you can tell, never will). Bus driving is steady, it pays decently, and it's simple. In fact, you might decide to stick with it.
At least, those were your thoughts until you saw the wreck of a school bus impaled on the swings. You haven't even been here half a year, and now you might have to leave again if this, this antibus cult thingy ends up destroying all the buses. There goes your money, your job, your benefits package… You must stop them.

Your power lies in your observation skills, your voice, and your vote. 

You win when all Mafia are eliminated, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is [url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]here[/url]. Play towards your win condition, and good luck![/quote][quote="Bus Mechanic (Doctor)"]Hello, [player]!

You are the [color=#00f752]Bus Mechanic (Doctor)[/color]. 

You used to be the average local mechanic in an average-sized town in average suburbs, but as the population boomed, grander and fancier mechanic shops sprang up, and you started to lose business. Although you still had some faithful customers, a few repairs every few months wouldn't do, so when some enterprising individual offered to buy the shop to convert into a restaurant, you assented, albeit reluctantly. 
As you were considering your options, you received a curiously specific email offering you a job as a bus mechanic. The pay seemed good, and driving a bus in addition to the usual repair job didn't seem too hard. So you packed your bags, said goodbye to your old mechanic shop one last time, and headed out west, to Wyoming.

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then take in the bus for repairs for the night, thus preventing it from possibly being stolen and destroyed. You can save your own bus, but you cannot do this two nights in a row. 

You win when all Mafia are eliminated, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is [url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]here[/url]. Play towards your win condition, and good luck![/quote]
[quote="No-Longer-Bored Driver (Tracker)"]Hello, [player]!

You are the [color=#00f752]No-Longer-Bored Driver (Tracker)[/color]. 

Your job is rather dull. Driving around all day can make one tired, but you couldn't find another job, so you're stuck. Most days, you just end up reading whatever you can get your hands on--novels, newspapers, biographies--you don't care, and you don't usually pay too much attention anyway. 
When you received word of buses strangely flying through the air and crashing like meteors you had been in the middle of a mystery novel in which average people discover amazing secrets and catch notorious criminals… it [i]might[/i] have gotten to your head. So you've decided to try it out. After all, what's the worst that can happen? The perpetrators aren't dangerous--you think--and your job has been boring you all year long. You're not going to let that chance pass and just stick to some bus driver meetings.

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then [i]discreetly[/i] track them and find out who that player visits or targets, if any, that night. 

You win when all Mafia are eliminated, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is [url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]here[/url]. Play towards your win condition, and good luck![/quote]
[quote="Reckless Driver (Jailkeeper)"]Hello, [player]!

You are the [color=#00f752]Reckless Driver (Jailkeeper)[/color]. 

To be honest, you're probably the only one out of the bus drivers who truly enjoys their job. You're not just satisfied with it, you love it! After all, what's more fun than wheeling in a hulk of yellow metal all over the place all day long? The others don't quite understand your ecstasy, but, undaunted, you always volunteer for the longest routes and the toughest roads. Even the rowdiest kids can't ruin the thrill, because you're here, roaring over the highways, and that's all that matters. 

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then whisk them away in their bus for a midnight ride in an attempt to perhaps instill in them your own enthusiasm for buses, thus preventing them from being targeted or targeting anyone that night. You cannot target yourself with this ability. 

You win when all Mafia are eliminated, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is [url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]here[/url]. Play towards your win condition, and good luck![/quote]
[quote="Student Driver (Cop)"]Hello, [player]!

You are the [color=#00f752]Student Driver (Cop)[/color]. 

Out of all your fellow bus drivers, you like to think you have the brightest foreseeable future. You don't plan on sticking to this job, no. You're just here to make money--enough money to send yourself to a police academy and fulfill your childhood dream of becoming a police officer. You know it might take a while: that's why you started early and have seized every available resource at every turn. And now here's your chance! There are bad guys on the loose, outrageously wrecking without remorse government property, paid for by tax dollars, [i]and[/i], most importantly, your means of income, your only way to make it into the police academy. They must be stopped. 

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then put into practice all that you know so far about policing, investigating them and uncovering their alignment. You may also receive a "No result" if your action is prevented from resolving. 

You win when all Mafia are eliminated, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is [url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]here[/url]. Play towards your win condition, and good luck![/quote]
[quote="Paranoid Driver (1-shot Bulletproof)"]Hello, [player]!

You are the [color=#00f752]Paranoid Driver (1-shot Bulletproof)[/color]. 

You've been paranoid your entire life, anxious, nervous of what is to come. The solution? You overthink things--or maybe that's the problem. You've heard stories of you running to your mother as a young child, worried whether your birthday would really come or whether your friends would really come to the party. At any rate, that might be why you took the job of a bus driver. Something simple, repetitive, un-worrisome, and maybe just a [i]little[/i] bit enjoyable. But then, when you hear about the whole stuffed bus fiasco, it all comes back. Your anxiety. You feel the urge to do something, but you're not sure what. Eventually, you decide to install security for your bus. [i]Bus security?[/i] You don't care how stupid it sounds: it works, and that's all that matters for you.

You and your bus with its hastily installed security system will be able to survive one attempted nightkill, but since you did not have enough time to fully secure the bus, if you are targeted again after that, you will be eliminated. You are not immune to the daily hang, but your ability cannot be roleblocked. You do not know when you lose your bulletproof vest. 

You win when all Mafia are eliminated, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is [url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]here[/url]. Play towards your win condition, and good luck![/quote]
[quote="Member of the Coalition Against Buses (Mafia Goon)"]Hello, [player]!

You are a [color=red]Member of the Coalition Against Buses (Mafia Goon)[/color].

You loathe buses. Or more accurately you abhor, abominate, despise, detest, execrate, revile, scorn, spurn, [i]and[/i] hate them. For what reason? Who knows. Hate is illogical. So here you are, undercover as a bus driver for several months now--such a repulsive career--and it's finally time. The plan of years in making now flies into action:  
You'll start small, take over this little town in Wyoming, and eliminate all buses by catapulting them! Catapulting them--it had been such a brilliant idea: this way no one will know where to start looking for you, and you won't have to clean up after yourself. You just need to stay hidden until all resistance has been eliminated. From here, if you succeed, the secret Coalition Against Buses has plans to take over the world and purge all buses… school buses, public buses, ancient buses, toy buses… And you can't wait. 

Your partner is [player] ([role]), and you may discuss with them at night in [url=http://bitly.com/98K8eH]this QuickTopic[/url] while you are alive. Together, you may decide who will use the factional kill to eliminate which player each night.

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal is to stay hidden and kill Town before they find and hang you. The game thread is [url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]here[/url].
Play towards your win condition, and good luck![/quote]
[quote="Head of the Coalition Against Buses (Mafia Roleblocker)"]Hello, [player]!

You are a [color=red]Head of the Coalition Against Buses (Mafia Roleblocker)[/color]. 

When your grudge first started, you aren't sure, but since you were a child you had hated buses. School buses were stuffy and dirty. Public buses were always late. Tour buses? Well you relented and gave them a chance once and ended up getting your face whacked with all sorts of low hanging objects while riding atop it. But the breaking point came when your favorite aunt was run over by a bus… With the money she left you, you started to plot vengeance--against all buses. 
You started the secret Coalition Against Buses, located the perfect place from which to launch--a small town just setting up its school bus system--and prepared, prepared, and triple prepared. It's your chance. You can't afford to lose it, so you'll go to any extreme to ensure the successful debut of your Coalition Against Buses. The realization of your dream of a world with no buses is so, so close… 

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then break into that person's house, drug them, rendering them unconscious for the night. Thus you will roleblock them and prevent their night actions, if any, from resolving. 

Your partner is [player] ([role]), and you may discuss with them at night in [url=http://bitly.com/98K8eH]this QuickTopic[/url] while you are alive. Together, you may decide who will use the factional kill to eliminate which player each night. You can perform both the factional kill and your roleblock simultaneously. 

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal is to stay hidden and kill Town before they find and hang you. The game thread is [url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]here[/url].
Play towards your win condition, and good luck![/quote][/hide]
[/hide]

January 1, 20XX · Thursday · Dawn · The Death of the Stuffed Bus · A Journal Excerpt


Dear Journal,

I was swinging on the swing this morning in the empty schoolyard when a stuffed bus hit me in the face. I didn't see it coming, and it dropped out of nowhere!

I didn't know what it was at first, but I yelled and fell off the swing. It hit me just as I swang up really high, so I was kind of dizzy when I got up. There was no one around, though. I crawled over to the tree because my back hurt, and swinging was ruined anyway. I didn't want anything else to hit me in the face. And I double checked just to make sure. No one was around, which was really creepy. That was when the bus came.

It wasn't stuffed this time. But when I was on the other side of the tree checking just to be sure, there was a really big crash, and when I turned around I saw a big school bus--a real one, yellow and striped with a stop sign on the side--stuck on the swings! It was absolutely wrecked. The stuffed bus was ruined too. It was kind of sticking out of the side of the bus on the ground, smushed under a swing, and its fluff was leaking out. It didn't feel that fluffy when it hit me.

I think I started to try to run after a bit. Because if there were two buses, there might be more. I think I might have tripped a few times on the way, but I made it to the house by the school. My house was closer, on the other edge of the schoolyard, but that was the side the bus came from. I rang the doorbell, and a lady opened the door. She was the person who had convinced the school to establish a bus system in the first place. She called the police, they asked me some questions, and then I was sent home.

I couldn't figure out anymore, and my parents made me stay home. So I just played video games for the rest of the day.

Scipio1



Scipio1 has been clobbered in the face by a stuffed bus. He was the kid on the swing (Co-moderator).

Dawn has started 12:15 EST January 1, 2021. Day will start once everyone has read the rules and confirmed (the confirm rule can be found by thoroughly reading the suggested sections of the rules: Game Rules, Conduct Guidelines, and Setup).

You may now post. ^.^

She was the person who had convinced the school to establish a bus system in the first place. She called the police, they asked me some questions, and then I was sent home.

I couldn't figure out anymore, and my parents made me stay home. So I just played video games for the rest of the day.

[i]Scipio1[/i]



[b]Scipio1 has been clobbered in the face by a stuffed bus. He was the [color=#3d70ff]kid on the swing (Co-moderator)[/color].

[color=red]Dawn has started 12:15 EST January 1, 2021. Day will start once everyone has read the rules and confirmed (the confirm rule can be found by thoroughly reading the suggested sections of the rules: Game Rules, Conduct Guidelines, and Setup).[/color]

You may now post. ^.^

post_rendered:
Hi everyone!

I am benjaminsz, one of your experienced players for this game. This is my first game after a long hiatus I took since early 2020, so I might be a bit rusty and especially I might be unaware of any major meta-shifts that have occurred since then, but I am here anyway and ready to play! Feel free to ask me questions about anything related to the game or how it is played. I have one immediate warning for you however, and I really cannot stress this enough:

DO. NOT. BELIEVE. SOMETHING. SOLELY. BECAUSE. AN. EXPERIENCED. PLAYER. SAID. IT.

This is the number one way that newbie games are lost, or at least it was during the 60-odd game period where I was active. One or more new players assume anything that an experienced player says must be true because they are experienced, and then that experienced player turns out to be mafia. It doesn't always happen, but it happens enough that I felt like I should put this warning. By the same token, you shouldn't assume a player is town solely because they say that they think you are town. Anyway, I expect to not be terribly active until my break ends on Monday, but I will make sure not to fall too far behind. I'm not really in the mood to RVS vote right now, so I won't (although I will say, I am fairly certain that, for the average possible swing and the average possible bus collision scenario, you are unlikely to stay on the swing )

post_rendered:
January 1, 20XX · Thursday · Day Start · NotTM a Complaint · A Letter


To: Socoobo TheBossTM
5989 UnpaidIntern Dr.
Unpaid Intern Central, TM 63890

First off, sir, I would like to clarify that I think that the UnpaidInternsTM UnhiringTM program is flawless. I have no complaints about it. Working as an Unpaid InternTM for an elementary school in an isolated town in Wyoming is very, very interesting. But I am in a perplexing predicament right now, and I hope you will allow me to retract my contract of five years. Please, sir, before you set aside this letter, read in full the account of my circumstances.

As you know, this is my second year in the UnhiringTM program. My first year went well: I worked hard and earned nothing, to the credit of UnpaidInternCentralTM. At the end of that year, as I was checking records, I came upon an attendance issue, whereupon I suggested the establishment of a school bus system. I gave several reasons for its institution, and the plan went through. However, due to resistance from the townspeople who protested the consequent tax increase, the program could only be gradually implemented. I thus was promoted to the UnhiredTM head of the system of 10 bus drivers, myself included, at the beginning of this school year.

This morning some incredible thing happened. Bus number 1, my bus, was found impaled on the swing set in the schoolyard, by which my lodgings are immediately situated. A kid was out there at that time and ran to my door. I called the police immediately, and they came over. It appears that this kid was on the swing when a stuffed bus knocked him off, and soon after that my school bus presumably came out of the air and smashed into the swings. It sounds ridiculous, but the wreckage was before my eyes.

I was later arrested for the demolished bus being my bus, although I told them that it might have been any of the other drivers, so I am currently under custody. However, that is not the greatest issue, for I am sure I will be cleared soon. Rather, given the ardent nature of the town's protests against the buses, it may be better that I am in jail for now--not that there is any physical threat, but so I will not have to bother too much about the ongoing state of affairs. I sometimes doubt my work is worth the trouble (I assure you that this is not because I am UnpaidTM).

Such is my case: I have worked for nearly two years in the UnhiringTM under a contract I did not necessarily agree to nor was aware of, and I have done more than my part of the contract, having been assigned to other roles here. Now the bus program I started has resulted in some antagonism against me. Hence, I request that the contract be terminated and that I be allowed to leave after this issue is resolved. I am sure that the school will offer no objection, and the bus program would face less resistance in my absence. Furthermore, withdrawing me will not damage the reputation or standing of the UnhiringTM program in any way, but I would think it would rather set it on a better footing.

I hope you will consider my request.

Sincerely,

HannahW
On January the First, the year 20XX



HannahW has been arrested. She was the UnhiredTM founder of the bus system (Moderator).

Day 1 has started 18:30 EST January 5, 2020. Deadline is in 14 days at 18:30 EST on January 19, 2020. Majority hang is off.

Now the bus program I started has resulted in some antagonism against me. Hence, I request that the contract be terminated and that I be allowed to leave after this issue is resolved. I am sure that the school will offer no objection, and the bus program would face less resistance in my absence. Furthermore, withdrawing me will not damage the reputation or standing of the Unhiring[sup]TM[/sup] program in any way, but I would think it would rather set it on a better footing.

I hope you will consider my request. 

Sincerely,

[i]HannahW
On January the First, the year 20XX[/i]

While looking back at andy's posts, I realized that don't quite match. Usually newbscum partners would defend each other from attacks, but when people started voting tw, andy jumped on the tw wagon. I don't see newbscum bussing each other D1. But his actions are still indeed scummy. I'm moving him up to my heavy scumlean, but not scumread
BTW, for newbies, here are some words that experienced players often use.
conf town or lock town - you are 300% sure that the person is town
town read or tr - You are pretty sure this person is town, but you can't be 100% sure
townlean or tl - You think this person is leaning towards town, but you are not sure they're town
null or nai - Not enough information or this person does not lean to either side
scumlean or sl - You think this person is leaning towards scum, but you are not sure if they're town
scumread or sr - You are pretty sure this person is scum, but you can't be 100% sure
conf town or lock town - you are 300% sure that the person is scum
