January 1, 20XX · Thursday · End of Day 1 · Impromptu Interrogation · Transcript


Name: twsaving2
Suspected of: Catapulting school buses

Interrogator: Good evening… what's your name?

Subject: twsaving2.

Interrogator: T.W… Saving-tu...? Nevermind. May I just address you as TW?

TW: Yeah, sure.

Interrogator: Okay… And, um, why are you here?

TW: Huh?

Interrogator: Let me rephrase this. What do you do, and what's your relation to the people who brought you here?

TW: I am a… driver. I drive school buses--yeah, I drive. The others are also drivers.

Interrogator: Ah! So you are here… because of the impaled bus incident this morning! …right?

TW: ...Yes? They think I'm suspicious and are determined that the person who launched the buses is among them… But, uh, don't you know this already? I want to get out of here.

Interrogator: Sorry. Uh… Honestly, the lot of you drivers just crammed in and you were shoved at me and I was told to interrogate you… I didn't catch the charge though.

[a bang on the one way glass]

Interrogator: Er… Give me a second.

[a chair scrapes against the floor and the door opens]
[faintly]
Interrogator: Hey, why are they here.
Other officer: They won't leave. [inaudible]... I tried.
Interrogator: [inaudible] just… stay… get [groan]... be quiet ok. But wait one question… [inaudible]
[inaudible reply]
Interrogator: What?! You have [inaudible] evidence?
[inaudible reply]

January 2, 20XX · Friday · End of Night 1 · School Bus Strikes Again · Wyoming Post


Written by W Thoughts.

At 7:00 this morning, as local bus driver benjaminsz was driving to work, a school bus hurtled through the air and smashed into the front of his car. He had just exited the highway to town and was crossing the bridge over the highway, heading toward Main Street, when this accident--if it can be called one--occurred.

The bridge fell through, blocking the westbound highway route to town, creating a relatively sizable traffic jam. Those coming from the east fared better. Although they could not cross over at the collapsed bridge, they could still exit and cross the highway a few miles down the road.

benjaminsz was taken to the hospital this morning in an ambulance. Given the size and nature of the wreckage, many are surprised that he was not instantaneously killed. At the moment he is in a coma, and doctors are unsure whether he will survive.

This has been the second incident of a school bus falling from the sky into our Wyoming hometown, and the first incident with a casualty.

The original incident occurred just yesterday morning and was witnessed by an elementary school boy. More details can be found in this article.

Local residents are extremely worried and agitated about these recent developments. Many have concerns about property damage and more far-reaching obstructions once winter break is over. One local resident, the mother of the boy who witnessed the first school bus crash, commented, "I really don't feel safe anymore. None of us do. It sounds ridiculous out of context, but we're all afraid that the next one will be us. And if this isn't stopped, I don't think any of us will be comfortable sending our kids to school when their school buses are literally being launched at people."

Others cite these incidents as a startling example of the negative effects of the recently implemented bus system. "Just look at this mess," a local follower of the Coalition Against Buses told reporters, "this is exactly what we warned you about. The buses are just going to consume tax dollars. They've already destroyed the playground, two buses have been wrecked, the highway has been blocked, the bridge is estimated to cost thousands of dollars to repair… And if you ask me, those bus drivers who receive pay by lazing around all day? They should be checked." He is not alone in these sentiments.

Many others have also recently expressed approval of the recent arrest of HannahW, who founded the bus system and is expected soon to be released. "I don't care if she's innocent, she started this whole ordeal," one resident argued, "If she won't stay in jail, then she can leave!"

Likewise the sentiment against the bus drivers had been growing increasingly hostile. However, the first casualty of a falling bus being a bus driver has caused local residents to think twice. Others are not convinced.

In the meantime, the police are continuing to pursue their leads after the successful capture of one culprit last night.



benjaminsz has been hospitalized. He was a Reckless Driver (Town Jailkeeper).

Night 1 has ended and Day 2 has started on January 22, 2021 - 7:30 PM. Day 2 ends in 14 days on February 5, 2021 - 6:30 PM.
You impatient peoples can post now.

post_rendered:
January 2, 20XX · Friday · End of Day 2 · Bunnynapping · Report


Operation Duckify Easter, Mission #031
Filed by the Honorable Duckling


I infiltrated the enemy base as instructed, and procured the target information. The details of the enemy mission number 0986 are as follows:

Objective: Find an Easter Bunny
Agent: Easter Egg Henchman #2

There was nothing else.

In accordance with the obtained information, I stalked Easter Egg Henchman #2. I was careful to keep behind the bushes and trees this time, although people still ended up chucking bread at me. The only incident that occurred was when I hid behind the fence in someone's backyard. Apparently there was a dog, and I narrowly escaped into the house, which resulted in narrowly avoiding becoming dinner. Nevertheless, I escaped by my surpassing skills and managed to swoop out of the house, in between the wheels of a car coming up the driveway, and into a bush.

The first day of stalking was quite eventless. I watched Easter Egg Henchman #2 go door to door only to have each one frantically slammed in his face. The second day, he must have received some new instructions from his supervisors, for he tried snatching one man as he was departing from the supermarket, but the man snatched Easter Egg Henchman #2 up instead and hurtled him quite a distance. He landed in the bushes, but had he not, he would have become a crackhead, thus ruining any chance of us potentially recruiting him. Even as an egghead, though, he follows his orders so rigorously that I doubt we would have any chance anyway. I am quite astonished as to how he survived through the second day without a scratch, even successfully completing his mission.

As evening drew near on the second day, Easter Egg Henchman #2 passed by the elementary school. I believe he meant to completely neglect it, for it is usually deserted during the winter holidays, but as he ventured near, a small crowd of six people surged out, accosting amidst them a girl with a bright greenish blue hat. However, seeing the egg head of Easter Egg Henchman #2, they scrambled for the door. Easter Egg Henchman grabbed the last of them, who was the girl, since those accosting her had instinctively pushed her away, and ran off.

The people popped back out of the door in a second. One tried to give chase, but got 5 feet before he realised that it was already too late. Instead they resorted to yells, telling Easter Egg Henchman #2 to come back and asking for his identity and his business and whatnot. But all he replied with was a sneeze, which I believe was an accident.

As I followed Easter Egg Henchman #2, I almost tripped on the fellow duck who was assigned to following the henchman who was following me as I followed the Easter Egg Henchman #2. I think he needs a bit more training.

I have submitted with this report a photo I took as the central incidents of this report occurred as evidence for their validity.



Madelyn has been bunnynapped. She was a Bus Driver (Vanilla Townie).

Day 2 has ended and Night 2 has started on January 29, 2021 - 21:45 EST. Night 2 ends in 3 days on February 1, 2021 - 21:45 EST.

post_rendered:
January 3, 20XX · Saturday · End of Night 2 · Once Upon a Time · A Folktale


Narrated by Great Granny

"Once upon a time there dwelt in the land of Wyoming a legend named aroodman.

He was an ancient man, wise and full of years, but, most importantly, he was a bus driver. He heralded the first wave of great yellow and black buses across the Wyoming landscapes, carrying out his honorable mission of transporting children to the brick and mortar school erected for the purpose of their acquisition of incredible knowledge that would destine some of them for great places. He -- yes, he, aroodman -- was among the first to do that, so great he was.

Then one day, evil came over land. A secret cult lurking in the shadows and recesses sought to overturn the vehicles of such incredible learning and power. In utter wickedness they plotted and decided… to eliminate these honorable, striped yellow buses once and for all. With their dark arts they concocted a contraption that would launch these vehicles through the sky in all their glory and then plummet them to their terrible demise.

Oh how mournful were those days! Yet aroodman and the others gathered together in courage to oust the evil from their midst. After great deliberations, they managed to catch one of those devious, demonic conspirators. Spurred on by their good success and in name of their incapacitated colleague, benjaminsz, they, the next day, chose to condemn Madelyn. Alas, alas, their choice was wrong. And thus the fall of aroodman came, impending with the dooming night of bewilderment and regret and despair.

That night -- oh I scarcely can speak of it -- as the great aroodman was taking his rest to best be able to handle with wisdom the weighty decisions of the coming day, a bus came hurtling through the air, flashing a last dutiful farewell in the moonlight, and crashed straight into aroodman's resting place. The gentle and brilliant rays of the morning sun found him gone. His body could not be found anywhere, yet no one has seen or heard any word of him since.

Legend says that the Almighty Bus God took note of the great aroodman's dutiful services and shielded him from evil forever by taking him with him into the unending sky above..."



aroodman has vanished. He was a Bus Driver (Vanilla Townie).

Night 2 has ended and Day 3 has started on February 1, 2021 - 22:00 EST. Day 3 ends in 14 days on February 15, 2021 - 22:00 EST.

post_rendered:
(image coming soon)


January 4, 20XX · Sunday · End of Night 3 · Peppermint Winter · Narrative


The world is shawled with snow as I step out -- free, finally. It took them long enough. The sun isn't quite up yet, and there still linger a few stars in the sky. I pause in the quiet and look up for a while -- the air is cold, but not frigid, and still. Where do I go?

Home, if it can be called that, the rooms I rented, they stand empty several blocks away, by the school. It's a wonder how a few days of isolation, partly self-made, can clear things up. The school is simply the school now. If the residents refuse to allow the school bus system, it's their loss. I tried, and they refused. As I stroll along the unshoveled sidewalk, the houses are just houses, the people are just people, and I note that I ultimately don't belong here. If The BossTM has received my letter -- or even if he doesn't -- I'm leaving soon. The only things I and this town share are the sky and the air and the ground and the snow, or so I think.

Even then, I take a detour about the school when I near my lodgings. The sky is a wintry golden, and I figure that I might see the sunrise through. There is little else to do. Any bus troubles are scarcely my business anymore. But as I watch the clouds I see a well-known shadow pass through the sky. A bus. I rush to the school bus lot. Where there were 10 buses, only three remain. Gone are any washings of my hands free of this business. Those buses I toiled for, and now some… people… are trashing them. Bus number 6… 3… 9… Only those of Osmium, cherry, and violet remain. Seven gone, and it probably isn't even the work of the townspeople. They complain but do little, so in a huff I stick the key into the frosted lock of my front door and call the police… little else I can do.

Day 4 has ended. Congrats to the not-undercover bus drivers for winning. Impatient peoples may post now. Patient peoples may also post.

benjaminsz eventually recovered from his coma and was hailed as a state hero and got to meet the president, to his utter confusion.

aroodman actually barely escaped the collapse of his house and hopped into his car and drove without stopping all the way back to Iowa, in which, to this day, he remains, oblivious to his legendary fame in Wyoming.
andy09 left town and pursued a relatively successful bus driving career elsewhere.
fallinqsnow- stuck around for a while and was rehired as the new head of the bus system when it was voluntarily reimplemented a year after HannahW's departure.
Osmium_ and violetmx12 received the AoPSTownWinTM award.
HannahW left that summer after smoothing things out.
Scipio1 was recruited by HannahW to be the newest Unpaid InternTM and went with her to Unpaid Intern CentralTM that summer.

conf town or lock town - you are 300% sure that the person is town
town read or tr - You are pretty sure this person is town, but you can't be 100% sure
townlean or tl - You think this person is leaning towards town, but you are not sure they're town
null or nai - Not enough information or this person does not lean to either side
scumlean or sl - You think this person is leaning towards scum, but you are not sure if they're town
scumread or sr - You are pretty sure this person is scum, but you can't be 100% sure

post_rendered:
About shooting the BP:

It sounds unintuitive, because getting shot is the literal purpose of the BP in theory, but suppose you're going into night with 2 VTs, a BP and a goon.

Goon shoots VT: Instant LyLo with one mech clear

vs.

Goon shoots BP: Mylo with one mech clear, day skipped
Goon shoots BP again: LyLo with 0 mech clears

In fact, in that scenario, town shouldn't even skip MyLo, because you know the remaining scum will just shoot the BP again anyway, so passing the day doesn't actually narrow PoE for town.

Of course, there's also the question of whether the BP or a VT is more likely to misvote in LyLo. That's a whole other realm.
post_canonical:
About shooting the BP:

It sounds unintuitive, because getting shot is the literal purpose of the BP in theory, but suppose you're going into night with 2 VTs, a BP and a goon.

Goon shoots VT: Instant LyLo with one mech clear

vs.

Goon shoots BP: Mylo with one mech clear, day skipped
Goon shoots BP again: LyLo with 0 mech clears

In fact, in that scenario, town shouldn't even skip MyLo, because you know the remaining scum will just shoot the BP again anyway, so passing the day doesn't actually narrow PoE for town.

Of course, there's also the question of whether the BP or a VT is more likely to misvote in LyLo. That's a whole other realm.

conf town or lock town - you are 300% sure that the person is town

null or nai - Not enough information or this person does not lean to either side
scumlean or sl - You think this person is leaning towards scum, but you are not sure if they're town
